title: Aichivirus real-time RT-PCR 2007 method
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"evfhc","text":"A real-time assay to detect Aichivirus A (AiV-A) in human samples.  It is based in the 3'UTR region and has been employed in our laboratory since 2007.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":28,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cbqd","text":"If using a different brand or model of real-time thermocycler, check the concentration of ROX is adequate.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5rgob","text":"Method assumes the user is familiar with the thermocycler and software used to run the protocol.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Reagents SuperScript™ III Platinum™ One-Step qRT-PCR Kit Life Technologies Catalog #11732088

Reaction Set-up Assay has been used on both a Rotor-Gene 6000 / Rotor-Gene Q 5-plex using 100-place rotor discs and a ABI 7500 Fast real-time machine. Total reaction volume is 20µL. Prepare sufficient for number of reaction plus a 'dead volume' usually 2 extra. Adjust as necessary if using a robotic dispenser. %s %s Dispense 15µL to each reaction well. Add 5µL of template, extracted RNA, controls or NTC (nuclease-free water). Total reaction volume is 20µL

Result analysis The definition used for a satisfactory positive result from a real-time fluorogenic PCR should include each of the following: A sigmoidal curve – the trace travels horizontally, curves upward, continues in an exponential rise and followed by a curve towards a horizontal plateau phase A suitable level of fluorescence intensity as measured in comparison to a positive control (y-axis) A defined threshold (C T ) value which the fluorescent curve has clearly exceeded (Fig.1 arrow) and which sits early in the log-linear phase and is <40 cycles A flat or non-sigmoidal curve or a curve that crosses the threshold with a C T value >40 cycles is considered a negative result NTCs should not produce a curve a Figure 1 . Examples of satisfactory sigmoidal amplification curve shape when considering an assay’s fluorescent signal output. The crossing point or threshold cycle (C T ) is indicated (yellow arrow); it is the value at which fluorescence levels surpass a predefined (usually set during validation, or arbitrary) threshold level as shown in this normalized linear scale depiction. LP-log-linear phase of signal generated during the exponential part of the PCR amplification; TP-a slowing of the amplification and accompanying fluorescence signal marks the transition phase; PP-the plateau phase is reached when there is little or no increase in fluorescent signal despite continued cycling.
creator: Judy A Northill
